2604.07126.title: Self-Discovered Intention-aware Transformer for Multi-modal Vehicle Trajectory Prediction
2604.07126.paper_summary: Predicting vehicle trajectories plays an important role in autonomous driving and ITS applications. Although multiple deep learning algorithms are devised to predict vehicle trajectories, their reliant on specific graph structure (e.g., Graph Neural Network) or explicit intention labeling limit their flexibilities. In this study, we propose a pure Transformer-based network with multiple modals considering their neighboring vehicles. Two separate tracks are employed. One track focuses on predicting the trajectories while the other focuses on predicting the likelihood of each intention considering neighboring vehicles. Study finds that the two track design can increase the performance by separating spatial module from the trajectory generating module. Also, we find the the model can learn an ordered group of trajectories by predicting residual offsets among K trajectories.
2604.07126.paper_summary_zh: 預測車輛軌跡在自駕車和智能交通系統應用中扮演著重要角色。儘管多種深度學習算法被設計用於預測車輛軌跡，但它們對特定圖結構（例如，圖神經網絡）或明確的意圖標記的依賴限制了它們的靈活性。在本研究中，我們提出了一種基於純Transformer的網絡，考慮到其鄰近車輛的多模態。使用了兩條獨立的軌道。一條軌道專注於預測軌跡，而另一條則專注於考慮鄰近車輛的每個意圖的可能性預測。研究發現，這種兩條軌道的設計可以通過將空間模塊與軌跡生成模塊分開來提高性能。此外，我們發現該模型可以通過預測K條軌跡之間的殘差偏移來學習有序的軌跡組。
2604.07126.author: Diyi Liu et.al.
2604.07126.authors: Diyi Liu, Zihan Niu, Tu Xu, Lishan Sun
